response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates strong distributed-systems and backend expertise with recent exposure to agentic-AI infrastructure at Google, the resume lacks the critical hard requirements for this CTO role: zero fintech domain expertise, no demonstrated production experience operating multi-agent AI systems at scale, and no evidence of team leadership or organizational scaling (the role requires scaling teams 10→30+). His background is pure IC technical growth rather than executive leadership, and the recent agentic work at Google is too recent to establish the production track record needed for this high-stakes, regulated fintech context.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and real-time backend expertise: handled millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby, built event-driven architectures across multiple companies
Recent direct exposure to agentic-AI infrastructure at Google (MCP, agentic workflows) shows current alignment with the core technical area
Proven rapid full-stack adaptability across Node, TypeScript, DLang, C++ and multiple domains, suggesting ability to move quickly in new areas
concerns: Zero fintech infrastructure experience: no background in credit systems, payment flows, embedded finance, or regulated financial environments—this is a hard requirement
Limited production multi-agent AI track record: current Google role is too recent to demonstrate real-world operating experience with agentic systems; prior roles show no multi-agent work

No evidence of team leadership or organizational scaling: resume shows purely individual-contributor progression (Developer→Senior→Staff) with no indication of managing teams, hiring, or building technical leadership layers

key_factors.factor: No demonstrated multi-agent AI production operating experience
Distributed systems and backend infrastructure depth
